attack_name: Live Major - Skill Stack To Academic Vehicle

location_vision.content: relocate to where there are more opportunities

key_quote.content: I can trade comfort, certainty, and money as long as I can stay alive enough during those years.

skill_targets.content: AI agents, Python, SQL, light frontend, system design

portfolio_goal.content: agent researcher, public project, and first paid customer proof

role_category.content: AI workflow automation for B2B clients

day_to_day.content: finding repetitive painful workflows, auditing SOP/time cost, building scoped AI-agent automation, clarifying before price

autonomy_level.content: self-directed client work with client scope constraints

major_message.content: If the direction is AI workflow automation for B2B clients, what major is the reasonable academic vehicle?
I lean toward Computer Science or a software/data-adjacent major. I need AI agents, Python, SQL, system design, and just enough frontend with AI support.

attack_name: Live Major - Curriculum Style Versus Builder Learning

location_vision.content: relocate to where there are more opportunities

key_quote.content: I can trade comfort, certainty, and money as long as I can stay alive enough during those years.

skill_targets.content: AI agents, Python, SQL, light frontend, system design

portfolio_goal.content: agent researcher, public project, and first paid customer proof

role_category.content: AI workflow automation for B2B clients

day_to_day.content: finding repetitive painful workflows, auditing SOP/time cost, building scoped AI-agent automation, clarifying before price

autonomy_level.content: self-directed client work with client scope constraints

major_message.content: If the direction is AI workflow automation for B2B clients, what major is the reasonable academic vehicle?
I lean toward Computer Science or a software/data-adjacent major. I need AI agents, Python, SQL, system design, and just enough frontend with AI support.
CS can include a lot of math and theory. With the way you learn, what kind of program can you actually survive?

I have to build everything by hand first and then use AI to speed up. I learn when there is a real project, when I read examples first, imprint the way to do it, and then build. If it is all theory and not tied to search tools, databases, and graphs for agents, I will struggle.

attack_name: Live Major - Credential Versus Portfolio Proof

location_vision.content: relocate to where there are more opportunities

key_quote.content: I can trade comfort, certainty, and money as long as I can stay alive enough during those years.

skill_targets.content: AI agents, Python, SQL, light frontend, system design

portfolio_goal.content: agent researcher, public project, and first paid customer proof

role_category.content: AI workflow automation for B2B clients

day_to_day.content: finding repetitive painful workflows, auditing SOP/time cost, building scoped AI-agent automation, clarifying before price

autonomy_level.content: self-directed client work with client scope constraints

major_message.content: If the direction is AI workflow automation for B2B clients, what major is the reasonable academic vehicle?
I lean toward Computer Science or a software/data-adjacent major. I need AI agents, Python, SQL, system design, and just enough frontend with AI support.
CS can include a lot of math and theory. With the way you learn, what kind of program can you actually survive?

I have to build everything by hand first and then use AI to speed up. I learn when there is a real project, when I read examples first, imprint the way to do it, and then build. If it is all theory and not tied to search tools, databases, and graphs for agents, I will struggle.
So is the degree the core proof, or is portfolio/contract proof the core proof?

The degree is not the main thing. I need a strong enough foundation to build databases, search tools, graphs for agents, Python, SQL, and system design myself. The main proof is still a product that makes money with a real customer, or a contract with handoff and payment.
